other_observers: Cole Wild; Cole Wild spotted these birds. I am not aware of any other observations of these birds prior to May 22. Warren Finch reported seeing them (possibly with Ric Olson) on May 29.
specific_location: north side of County Road 48, about 1.2 mile east of CR 47, at inlet canal for Lower Latham Reservoir
description: Adults were very large long-legged birds with long necks and feathery rumps typical of cranes. Overall coloration of body was cinnamon-brown (color of tannic acid-stained water) with some gray. Forecrown was crimson red. Long, sharp bills.   Chick was covered in yellow down.Behaviors: Foraging in wet meadow as a family group (two adults, one chick)Call: Did not vocalizePlumage: Adults and fledgling
